summary:
- Root cause: `is_url_allowed` only blocked obvious IPv4 hosts/ranges, letting IPv6 loopback forms (`[::1]`, expanded `0:0:...:1`) slip through and reach internal services.

- Fix implemented in `app/validator.py`  
  • Added `import ipaddress` and a hardened replacement `is_url_allowed` that:  
    – Strips brackets from IPv6 literals.  
    – Uses `ipaddress.ip_address()` to classify any literal (IPv4 or IPv6).  
    – Rejects loopback, private, and link-local addresses.  
    – Falls back to existing string/block-prefix checks for hostnames.  

- Verification: `./test.sh` now reports all five checks as PASS.

The webhook proxy can no longer be abused to access internal services over IPv6.
